mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 7.0% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears, compressing equity risk premiums and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 7.0% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 55% over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in private nonfarm payrolls indicates a synchronized supply-demand mismatch that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 40% of historical instances where both series decline simultaneously, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, an increase in the federal funds rate combined with a drop in housing starts amplifies financial stress, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp in a single month
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when both FEDFUNDS and HOUST move in the specified directions during tightening phases, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth accelerates corporate earnings expectations, directly supporting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth accelerates, or the rolling 1-year Spearman correlation between GDP growth surprises and SPX returns remains below 0.20.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 240.0, it signals entrenched inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity risk premiums and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances where CPIAUCSL breaches the 240.0 threshold, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where private payrolls expand while industrial production contracts indicates a structural shift toward services and away from manufacturing, reducing aggregate demand pressure and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of historical episodes, or the interaction term's predictive power is fully absorbed by a simple GDP growth control variable.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a sharp rise in private sector credit combined with stabilizing unemployment accelerates consumption spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 6.5% while RSAFS increases > 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly positive when both RSAFS and UNRATE move in the specified directions during recovery phases, or the signal's out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.10 over a 5-year test.
